# Transcrição: They_Spelled_My_Name_Wrong...

- **Fonte:** They_Spelled_My_Name_Wrong...
- **Duração:** 02:44
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:44]

I have this one photo that pisses me off more than any other photo. I'll show it to you in just a second. Let me set the scene. Many of you don't know this about me, but my birth name isn't Vim. It's Kwan. That's right, Kwan. One of the reasons I changed my name when I was 15 was because people used to get my name wrong all the time. They used to call me Kwaaaang. That sound drove me crazy. Sometimes they would say it wrong and sometimes they simply didn't even remember my name, even though I was in their class for a whole damn year. So I changed my name legally to Vim and I hope that it was easier for people to remember, for people to pronounce and I was hoping that they could bloody spell it. Clearly someone was a little bit sensitive to people getting their name wrong. Last week on Facebook Memories, this photo appeared here. It's a photo from 2008, 15 bloody years ago and when I saw it again, it pissed me off, just like how it pissed me off on the day when I saw it in front of me. They spelled my name wrong. I got booked for an event as a magician and this was the sign in front of my dressing room. That's not how you spell my name. My name is spelled V-I-N-H-G-I-A-N-G. That night I made a massive fuss over the fact that they got my name wrong and to be honest, I was a little bit of a wanker that evening to the event organizer. I made a big deal out of it, even though it wasn't that big of a deal in the grand scheme of things, but to me it was a big deal. My whole life I was invisible. My whole life I didn't stand out. My whole life I was so easily forgettable. I was invisible. I was invisible because I didn't know how to communicate the value that I had inside. Sure, I had talents and gifts, but they all remained hidden because I didn't know how to bring them to life. As I learned how to communicate more effectively, I no longer remained a secret. This was the skill that changed everything for me. Let's just say they don't get my name wrong anymore. I sometimes go back and watch my old videos that I've made years ago and I feel really proud of myself because English is my third language. It's been one hell of a long and tough journey for me to get to where I am today with my communication skills and I share this with you because if you feel misunderstood or you feel invisible too, then join me for a master class where I'm going to share with you the core foundations of masterful communication. I wasn't going to do another master class this year, but so many of you missed my master class in October, so I'm putting together one final master class for 2023. Come join me. I've put the link in the comment section. Click that link to register and I can't wait to see you at the master class.
